description: US-15 Browse and Search the Customer List — Search by name, customer number or card number; filter by status, group and certificate expiry; both group sizes always visible. Restores what the Excel sheet did.

userStories.title: listCustomers query use case with filters
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to search and filter all customers, so I can answer questions away from the counter. Source: tasks/prd-us-15-customer-list.md §US-15.1
acceptanceCriteria: listCustomers(deps, {search?, status?, group?, certificate?, includeArchived?}) is implemented
search matches last name, first name (case- and diacritic-insensitive), an exact customer number, or a card number resolved to its holder
certificate accepts VALID | EXPIRING_SOON | EXPIRED, where expiring-soon is within 30 days of deps.clock.now() and the constant is documented
includeArchived defaults to false; archived customers appear only when it is true or status names ARCHIVED
Results carry customer number, name, group, status, derived counts, certificate expiry, reminder count and current card number
The result carries groupCounts {red, blue} for active customers independent of the current filter — asserted by a test that filters to Blue and still receives both
Default sort is ascending customer number
The use case performs no writes
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer list queries and indexes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the list filters implemented in SQL with the search normalisation shared with the archive search. Source: tasks/prd-us-15-customer-list.md §US-15.2
acceptanceCriteria: Filters are implemented in SQL, not by loading all rows and filtering in JavaScript; only the derived counts are computed per row after loading
Indexes exist on lastName, customerNumber, status and group
The normalised search column introduced for the archive search is reused — there is one search-normalisation approach in the codebase, not two
Integration test seeds around 50 synthetic customers across statuses and groups and asserts each filter's result set and the group counts
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/ if the schema changed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer list screen at /kunden
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want a dense searchable list, so the app can replace the spreadsheet I used to filter. Source: tasks/prd-us-15-customer-list.md §US-15.3
acceptanceCriteria: Route /kunden shows a dense table: number, name, group, status, counts, portions, price, certificate expiry, reminder count
A single search box handles name, customer number and card number, with a German placeholder saying so
Filter controls for status, group and certificate state, with filter state reflected in the URL so a view can be bookmarked
Both active group sizes are shown prominently above the table
Archived customers are hidden until a clearly labelled toggle is switched on; archived rows are visually distinct
Empty results render an explicit German message naming the active filters, never a blank table
Each row links to /kunden/[id]; status is conveyed by text plus colour, never colour alone
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: search and filter the customer list
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec covering search, filters and the group-balance figures. Source: tasks/prd-us-15-customer-list.md §US-15.4
acceptanceCriteria: Spec seeds customers across groups, statuses and certificate states
Spec asserts name search finds the right customer, a card number resolves to its holder, the blocked filter shows only blocked customers, and archived are hidden by default but appear when toggled
Spec asserts the group counts match the seeded data and do not change when a filter is applied
Spec asserts the URL carries the filter state and reloading restores the same view
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
